title: cornus mas f. aurea-elegantissima
intro: Cornus mas f. aurea-elegantissima, also known as the Golden Dogwood, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Cornaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The Golden Dogwood is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, and is also thought to have antioxidant and immune-boosting effects. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be made from its bark, leaves, and roots, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential uses.
